channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Grizzlies beat the Mavericks 120-106 to set up a 1st-round playoff series with the Thunder
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Ja Morant shrugged off a sprained right ankle to score 22 points and add nine assists and the Memphis Grizzlies beat the Dallas Mavericks 120-106 on Friday night to advance to the NBA playoffs.
item.dc:creator: CLAY BAILEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boston Marathon and city insist all are welcome. But some runners say politics will keep them home
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The Boston Marathon and Mayor Michelle Wu insisted on Friday that international runners and other foreign visitors remain welcome in the city and said there is no evidence that travel for this year’s race has fallen off in the face of increased border scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lindor hits 1st walk-off homer for Mets, 250th home run of career, in 5-4 win over Cardinals
item.description: Francisco Lindor turned on an inside cutter from Ryan Fernandez leading off the ninth inning and sent the ball soaring into Citi Field’s second deck in right field, 401 feet away.
item.dc:creator: Ronald Blum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Herro scores 30 points to lead Heat past Hawks 123-114 in OT in play-in and into NBA playoffs
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — After making NBA Play-In Tournament history by winning two road games, the Miami Heat won't be afraid of facing No. 1 seed Cleveland in the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Edman hits leadoff HR filling in for Ohtani and Yamamoto strikes out 10 as Dodgers beat Texas 3-0
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Yoshinobu Yamamoto matched his career high with 10 strikeouts over seven innings after Tommy Edman led off the game with a home run and the Los Angeles Dodgers beat the Texas Rangers 3-0 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Inside the numbers: Led by Cleveland and Oklahoma City, it was the season of the streak in the NBA
item.description: It was the year of the long winning streak in the NBA. There were five instances of teams winning 11 or more games in a row — three by Cleveland, two by Oklahoma City, and it's probably no coincidence that those two teams are the ones that earned No.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Lindblad joins Buhai atop the leaderboard in the LPGA Tour's JM Eagle LA Championship
item.description: Ingrid Lindblad took a share of the lead into the weekend at El Caballero Country Club in only the fourth start of her rookie season on the LPGA Tour.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former Cal, Monte Vista tight end Jack Endries commits to Texas after leaving Golden Bears
item.description: Monte Vista High football alum Jack Endries commits to Texas after two strong seasons at Cal, including freshman All-American and all-ACC honorable mention campaigns
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Shohei Ohtani absent from Dodgers for birth of first child
item.description: MLB superstar Shohei Ohtani is away from the LA Dodgers for the birth of his first child, manager Dave Roberts told reporters on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gregg Popovich has medical incident in restaurant, is resting at home, AP source says
item.description: San Antonio coach Gregg Popovich is doing well at home after needing medical attention earlier this week following an incident at a restaurant, a person with knowledge of the matter told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shohei Ohtani away from the Los Angeles Dodgers for birth of his first child
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Shohei Ohtani is away from the Los Angeles Dodgers for the birth of the two-way superstar’s first child. Manager Dave Roberts said before the Dodgers’ series opener Friday night in Texas that Ohtani was with his wife and going on MLB’s paternity list.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Spurs coach Gregg Popovich has medical emergency while recovering from stroke: report
item.description: As he continues to recover from a stroke he had late last year, Gregg Popovich reportedly experienced a medical emergency that landed him in a hospital Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill Belichick nabs 4-star receiver for UNC who was going to play for Deion Sanders at Colorado
item.description: Bill Belichick and UNC have brought in four-star receiver Adrian Wilson, who entered the transfer portal Wednesday after previously committing to Colorado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Haley Cavinder gets engaged to Cowboys star shortly after her sister's rumored breakup with Carson Beck
item.description: Former Miami basketball star Haley Cavinder announced Thursday she is engaged to Dallas Cowboys tight end Jake Ferguson after nearly two years of dating.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aaron Rodgers says he's not holding teams hostage as he ponders his future in the NFL
item.description: Aaron Rodgers is in no rush to make a decision about his future. The free-agent quarterback said during an appearance on “The Pat McAfee Show” on Thursday that he is “not holding anybody hostage" as he weighs whether to return for a 21st season. Rodgers was released by the New York Jets in March.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PGA star signs woman's leg after hitting it with errant tee shot
item.description: Billy Horschel found himself in an awkward moment Thursday at the RBC Heritage. He signed a woman's leg after he hit it with a tee shot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IHSA upholds policy on transgender athletes, seeks clarity on state conflict with Trump order
item.description: The Illinois High School Association this week said it would keep in place its policy to allow transgender athletes to compete in high school sports competitions, despite an executive order from President Donald Trump, because the order may conflict with state law.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: MLB ump leaves game after foul ball hits him in the face
item.description: Veteran umpire Hunter Wendelstedt instantly hit the ground after he took a direct hit from the line smash off the bat of New York Mets center fielder Tyrone Taylor in the seventh inning.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Off-road racer becoming one of most dominant drivers in desert
item.description: The Imperial Valley spans an area of roughly 4,400 square miles, and Brock Heger knows almost every inch of it.
item.dc:creator: Troy Hirsch, Fox 5

item.title: White Sox DFA Mike Clevinger, call-up Steven Wilson from AAA
item.description: The Chicago White Sox have designated Mike Clevinger for assignment and called up reliever Steven Wilson from Triple-A, while Wilson remains on the 40-man roster.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Warriors edge Grizzlies in Play-In Tournament thriller, clinch playoff spot
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors are officially going to the 2025 NBA Playoffs after a nail-biting 119-116 win over the Memphis Grizzlies on Tuesday night in San Francisco. The victory sets Golden State (48-34) up with a first-round matchup against the No.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Hendricks plans to come back stronger than ever
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - This was supposed to be the season Taylor Hendricks took a big step forward. After showing promising signs his rookie year, in which he averaged 7.3 points in 40 games, Hendricks was excited to be a big part of the Jazz rebuild.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Warriors host Memphis Grizzlies in NBA play-in game Tuesday at Chase Center
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (BCN) -- The Golden State Warriors missed a chance to clinch a National Basketball Association playoff spot in their last regular season game on Sunday but will get another chance when they host a play-in game Tuesday against the Memphis Grizzlies at Chase Center in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: Blackhawks prospect Ryan Greene makes his NHL debut at Montreal
item.description: Ryan Greene made his NHL debut with the Chicago Blackhawks on Monday night, playing 13 minutes in a 4-3 shootout win at Montreal, and is expected to play in their final game of the season at Ottawa on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Cubs can't hold lead, MLB-best Padres surge late to win opening game of 3-game series in San Diego
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The Cubs' West Coast gauntlet against two of the best teams in baseball rolls on this week, with two games left.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Keller's four points leads Utah past Nashville, 7-3
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (ABC4 Sports) - With nothing to play for but pride, the Utah Hockey Club is out to finish the season strong.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Garrett Crochet dazzles in return to South Side, takes no-no into the 8th in Red Sox victory
item.description: Garrett Crochet returned to the South Side with a vengeance, throwing seven and one-third innings and striking out eleven, but the Red Sox won the game 3-1 to avoid a sweep.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
